metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Identify and link (or inline) the 'dso content' source that defines the recall patterns and FP-guards — it is currently unreferenceable in the codebase.
findings.finding: The plan's enrichment is predicated on 'dso content' as the authoritative source for both recall patterns and FP-guards, but 'dso' appears nowhere in the codebase, docs, or any named file — it is an unverifiable assertion with no backing artifact that an implementer can reference.
findings.location: ## What — 'Enrich the llm-prompts overlay prompt with dso content: recall patterns AND FP-guards'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` (and any required eval_solver dispatch arm) to the plan's deliverables, or replace AC-2 with a testable alternative that works within the existing harness.
findings.finding: AC-2 asserts 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval', but no `code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` (or any `code-review-*.eval.yaml`) exists anywhere in the repo, and `eval_solver.run_case()` has no dispatch arm for a code-review overlay prompt ID — it would raise `ValueError` if called with 'code-review-llm-prompts'.
findings.location: ## Acceptance Criteria — AC-2: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval; does not over-suppress valid patterns.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` to the Files section and describe its fixture schema (recall cases with `input:` + expected fire, no-fire cases with expected empty findings), mirroring the existing plan-review-*.eval.yaml shape.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Either add an eval_solver dispatch arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs (or define an alternative execution path) so that `rebar prompt eval code-review-llm-prompts` can actually run the recall + no-fire fixtures, or scope that plumbing as an explicit dependency/prerequisite of this story.
findings.finding: AC2 ('Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval') asserts an eval execution capability that does not exist: eval_solver.run_case() has no dispatch arm for code-review overlay prompt IDs — it raises ValueError for any prompt_id that is not completion-verifier, ticket-quality, spec-alignment, or a plan-review criterion — and no code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml exists anywhere in the repo.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `code-review-llm-prompts` branch in eval_solver.run_case() analogous to how plan-review criteria are handled, or explicitly route this story through `rebar criteria eval` if the overlay is treated as a criterion — and note that dependency in the plan's Files or AC section.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the value vocabulary for each recall pattern family ('ui-artifact leakage', 'domain-mismatch', 'prompt/contract/output-schema drift') in the overlay instruction: what signals constitute a fire, what signals constitute suppression, and how each discriminates from a benign prompt change.
findings.finding: The three recall pattern families named in the plan ('ui-artifact leakage', 'domain-mismatch (reimplementing an existing prompt-library entry)', 'prompt/contract/output-schema drift') are referenced but their value vocabulary — what signals constitute a fire, what threshold separates a fire from a suppression — is defined nowhere in the plan or the existing codebase. The overlay prompt currently contains only a generic evidence-record shell with no domain-specific detection logic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a solver dispatch branch in `eval_solver.py` for the `code-review-llm-prompts` prompt (or document that the eval framework already handles code-review overlays via a different path), so `rebar prompt eval rebar-code-review-llm-prompts` can execute the recall + no-fire fixtures.
findings.finding: The eval spec file (`rebar-code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml`) required for the 'passing recall + no-fire eval' AC does not exist, and the eval solver (`eval_solver.py`) has no dispatch branch for `prompt_id == 'code-review-llm-prompts'` (or the `rebar-code-review-llm-prompts` langfuse id). The plan lists 'eval spec + fixtures' as a deliverable but does not mention adding the solver branch, meaning the eval infrastructure cannot execute the promised eval at all.
findings.suggested_fix: In eval_solver.py, add a branch analogous to the existing `ticket-quality` branch that invokes the code-review gate's Pass-1 over the case input and returns `{"findings": [...]}`, then add the `rebar-code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` spec under eval_specs/ with at least one `expect: finding` (recall) and one `expect: pass` (no-fire) case per named pattern family.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add or update an ADR / CLAUDE.md entry capturing the 'domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic verifier' architectural principle so future contributors can understand and enforce the boundary.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a locked architectural principle ('domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier') but does not call out a corresponding ADR, CLAUDE.md, or design-doc update to capture this decision for future maintainers.
findings.location: ## What — 'locked principle that domain rubric lives in the overlay, not the generic Pass-2 verifier'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a Files entry for `docs/adr/NNN-overlay-domain-rubric-boundary.md` (or update CLAUDE.md) that records: (1) the decision, (2) the rationale, and (3) the consequences — specifically that code-review-verify.md must remain domain-agnostic.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a concrete proving command for each AC (e.g., `run eval suite → all fixtures pass` for AC2; `grep -c 'domain rubric' code-review-verify.md → 0` for AC3; file-diff showing both pattern sections present for AC1).
findings.finding: No concrete proving command is described for any of the three acceptance criteria; an executor cannot produce evidence of completion beyond inspection.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the exact file path for the new eval spec (expected: `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml`) and confirm its `prompt:` key matches the workflow id `code-review-llm-prompts`, not the langfuse name `rebar-code-review-llm-prompts`.
findings.finding: The plan names 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay' in its Files section without specifying a concrete file path. The `load_eval_spec` mechanism resolves by prompt-id (`code-review-llm-prompts`) to `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` (packaged) or `.rebar/evals/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` (user override). Neither currently exists, and the plan provides no path, leaving the landing location ambiguous.
findings.location: Files section — 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay'
findings.suggested_fix: Add `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/code-review-llm-prompts.eval.yaml` to the Files section and note that the `prompt:` field in the YAML must be `code-review-llm-prompts` (the id used by `load_eval_spec`, matching `code-review.yaml:82`).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how eval fixture ground-truth is established independently of the overlay text being authored — e.g., fixtures sourced from labeled historical review examples, a pre-existing domain spec, or reviewed by a second author — so the eval is not a tautological self-authored oracle.
findings.finding: The eval fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay are authored by the same team writing the overlay, with no described independent ground truth. If fixtures are constructed by observing what the newly-written overlay fires on (or doesn't fire on), the eval becomes a self-authored oracle — it confirms the overlay does what it was written to do, not that it does the right thing. This locks in any systematic mis-calibration of the recall patterns or FP-guards.
findings.location: Files: 'eval spec + fixtures for the llm-prompts overlay (recall + no-fire cases)' / AC: 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the eval spec an explicit fixture sourcing policy: e.g., 'recall fixtures are drawn from N ≥ 5 past llm-prompts review findings labeled by a reviewer who did not write the overlay; no-fire fixtures are drawn from N ≥ 5 valid prompt-library entries that should not trigger any of the three recall patterns.' Document this policy in the eval spec header so reviewers can verify the oracle is independent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the eval threshold for AC2 explicitly (e.g., 'all named recall fixtures fire; all named no-fire fixtures are suppressed; zero regressions on existing overlay fixtures') so done-ness is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC2's completion threshold is undefined: 'passing recall + no-fire eval' and 'does not over-suppress valid patterns' contain no numeric or enumerable bar, making the criterion both ambiguous (E2) and unmeasurable (F1).
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'passing recall + no-fire eval' with: 'eval suite passes with 100 % recall on the three named pattern types (ui-artifact leakage, domain-mismatch, prompt/contract/output-schema drift) and 0 false fires on the no-fire fixture set.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Enumerate `.github/workflows/eval.yml` as an implicit consumer (auto-discovers all `eval_specs/*.eval.yaml`) and confirm the new spec will pass `validate_eval_spec(strict=True)` offline before landing.
findings.finding: The `.github/workflows/eval.yml` CI workflow auto-discovers ALL `src/rebar/llm/eval_specs/*.eval.yaml` files (lines 65 and 118) — both the offline `validate_eval_spec` discipline step and the live eval runner — and is an unenumerated consumer outside `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/`. Once the new eval spec lands in `eval_specs/`, it is automatically picked up and must pass `validate_eval_spec`. This consumer is not acknowledged in the plan.
findings.location: Files section / AC 'Lands with a passing recall + no-fire eval'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a one-sentence positive rationale for why inline prompt-text enrichment of `code-review-llm-prompts.md` is the right mechanism (e.g., 'the overlay prompt is the sole channel through which domain recall patterns reach the Pass-1 model at review time; eval fixtures validate but cannot substitute for it').
findings.finding: The plan states WHERE enrichment belongs (overlay, not verifier — the 'locked principle') but does not state WHY inline prompt-text enrichment of the existing overlay file is the right mechanism to achieve the recall/FP-discrimination goal. The positive rationale for the chosen approach is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify a fallback decision rule in the overlay instruction for the 'domain-mismatch' pattern: define how the reviewer should handle cases where the prompt-library state is uncertain or the match is below the confidence threshold (e.g., 'abstain and emit no finding when the overlap is partial').
findings.finding: The FP-discrimination guards ('both halves') are referenced in the plan but no fallback behavior is specified for the case where the diff is ambiguous — i.e., when a change touches a prompt file but it is unclear whether it constitutes 'domain-mismatch' (reimplementation of an existing library entry) vs. a legitimate new pattern. The overlay instruction (code-review-llm-prompts.md) contains no protocol for how to resolve ambiguous cases, no 'abstain' guidance, and no instruction on what a reviewer should do when the prompt-library corpus is not accessible via file tools.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
